name: linear_push

symptoms: a flat/tabletop object must be moved in XY without grasping, but the gripper misses, rides over the object, or pushes far past the target
the push line is kinematically reachable at the endpoints but dense Cartesian waypoints develop large mid-line IK errors
the object contacts the gripper and starts moving, then overshoots the requested target after the TCP command continues too far
trace_evidence: no gripper<->object contacts during PUSH means the TCP is too high, too far behind, or the gripper approach axis does not face the push direction
seeded push-line IK can lose the horizontal full-pose branch mid-line (observed max waypoint error 48.5 mm) even when start/end solve at ~2 mm
cube trajectory crosses the target and keeps moving while finger<->object impulses continue; shortening the commanded endpoint fixes the overshoot

when_to_apply: Use when a mobile-base AlohaMini Std/Pro robot should push a tabletop object along the table/floor plane to a target XY, especially when grasping is unnecessary or unreliable.
repair_strategy: Separate NAV and MANIP. First choose a manipulation station with the same teleport + physics settle + IK gate used for pick/place, keeping the root outside table/obstacle footprints. Orient the closed gripper with its approach axis parallel to the desired push direction and set the jaw axis perpendicular in the table plane. Move to a hover behind the object, descend to tip height near the object center z, then execute ~1 cm Cartesian IK waypoints along the horizontal push line. At each waypoint, seed from the previous solution but fall back to a shoulder-seed sweep when the residual jumps. Print object XY and contact pairs during execution; if there is no gripper contact, lower/reposition the line, and if the object overshoots, shorten the commanded TCP endpoint rather than changing the target.
code_sketch: start = object_center - approach_dir * backoff; start.z = object_center.z
for pt in cart_line(hover, start, spacing=0.01): q = full_pose_ik(pt, approach_dir, jaw_dir, seed=q)
for pt in cart_line(start, end, spacing=0.01):
if q.error is large: q = best_full_pose_seed_sweep(pt, approach_dir, jaw_dir)
step(act(station, q, closed_gripper)); print cube_xy and scene.get_contacts()
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro / ManiSkill AlohaMiniMultiYCB-v1 / 077_rubiks_cube: north-edge station (-0.049,+0.040,yaw=-90deg), closed gripper at z=0.729, 11 cm southward target; RESULT success=True, obj_final=(-0.1266,-0.4079,0.7292), target=(-0.1300,-0.4200), xy_err=12.6 mm, on_table=True
Failure repairs observed during validation: full-pose branch fallback reduced push-line max IK error 48.5 mm -> 13.9 mm; shortened TCP endpoint fixed overshoot from 76.4 mm final error -> 12.6 mm
